channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Ex-wife says ICE agent who killed man in Maine had racist beliefs, violent tendencies
item.description: Ashley Brouillette has identified her ex-husband, David Brouillette, as the officer who fatally shot Joan Sebastián Durán Guerrero in Biddeford, Maine, on Monday. She said she learned he was the officer responsible when he called her on Wednesday.
item.content:encoded:
Ashley Brouillette has identified her ex-husband, David Brouillette, as the officer who fatally shot Joan Sebastián Durán Guerrero in Biddeford, Maine, on Monday. She said she learned he was the officer responsible when he called her on Wednesday.

(Image credit: Yuki Iwamura)
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Romo

item.title: Headers at the World Cup
item.description: We look at a new soccer study that says "header" goals are becoming less frequent -- but more accurate -- in FIFA World Cup tournaments.
item.content:encoded: We look at a new soccer study that says "header" goals are becoming less frequent -- but more accurate -- in FIFA World Cup tournaments.
item.dc:creator: Zephyr Weinreich

item.title: Japan revises law on royals, keeps blocking women from throne
item.description: Japan has revised its laws to prevent the world's oldest hereditary monarchy from running out of heirs. But they have left in place a ban on female emperors, an idea which most Japanese support.
item.content:encoded: Japan has revised its laws to prevent the world's oldest hereditary monarchy from running out of heirs. But they have left in place a ban on female emperors, an idea which most Japanese support.
item.dc:creator: Anthony Kuhn

item.title: Argentina and Spain brace for world cup glory—or heartbreak
item.description: From Buenos Aires to Barcelona, fans are dreaming, waiting and wondering: who will lift the World Cup on Sunday?
item.content:encoded: From Buenos Aires to Barcelona, fans are dreaming, waiting and wondering: who will lift the World Cup on Sunday?
item.dc:creator: Miguel Macias

item.title: As competitive Senate map emerges, both Democrats and Republicans see a path to win
item.description: More than halfway through the primary elections, the map of competitive Senate races is becoming clearer, though there are still outstanding races to watch.
item.content:encoded: More than halfway through the primary elections, the map of competitive Senate races is becoming clearer, though there are still outstanding races to watch.
item.dc:creator: Maham Javaid

item.title: Following your aquarium fish back to its home in the Amazon
item.description: We trace the origins of the popular aquarium fish the cardinal tetra back to the Amazon to see how even the most remote corners of the world are transformed by the global economy.
item.content:encoded: We trace the origins of the popular aquarium fish the cardinal tetra back to the Amazon to see how even the most remote corners of the world are transformed by the global economy.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Guo

item.title: Link between raging wildfires and climate change is greater than ever
item.description: Human-caused climate change is driving many of the wildfires burning across Canada. What's harder to say is how much worse the planet is because of warming global temperatures.
item.content:encoded: Human-caused climate change is driving many of the wildfires burning across Canada. What's harder to say is how much worse the planet is because of warming global temperatures.
item.dc:creator: Ava Berger

item.title: Got a long summer reading list? NPR's 'Life Kit' helps you keep pages turning
item.description: Many people dream of extended summer reading time, but to really dig into books, you need steal any moment possible.
item.content:encoded: Many people dream of extended summer reading time, but to really dig into books, you need steal any moment possible.
item.dc:creator: Marielle Segarra

item.title: Cyclospora outbreak: FDA says do not eat shredded lettuce from Taco Bell in 5 states
item.description: Shredded lettuce served at Taco Bell in Michigan, Ohio, Kentucky, West Virginia and Indiana is linked to cyclospora outbreak. Investigation continues
item.content:encoded: Shredded lettuce served at Taco Bell in Michigan, Ohio, Kentucky, West Virginia and Indiana is linked to cyclospora outbreak. Investigation continues
item.dc:creator: Allison Aubrey

item.title: Trump promised evidence of election fraud. Experts say there was none
item.description: President Trump's primetime address Thursday warned of "shocking vulnerabilities" in U.S. elections, but many election experts say much of Trump's claims amount to old news.
item.content:encoded: President Trump's primetime address Thursday warned of "shocking vulnerabilities" in U.S. elections, but many election experts say much of Trump's claims amount to old news.
item.dc:creator: Franco Ordoñez
